mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path signals easier financial conditions that lowers the equity discount rate and supports S&P 500 gains over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because disinflation preserves real policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline whenever FEDFUNDS trends down over a 3-month window, or if the sign of the signal is negative in a majority of easing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold marks labor-market deterioration that raises recession risk and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment jumps are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the conditional average return is not materially below unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates strengthening domestic demand and housing activity, which supports near-term revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising HOUST does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in most matched recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO above its recent trend signals improving industrial output and easing supply-side slack, which lowers cyclical growth fears and supports S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if manufacturing upturn triggers do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal loses explanatory power across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains elevated while CPIAUCSL inflation is still sticky, real policy tightness rises and equity valuation pressure dominates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation trend is not falling
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation regimes do not produce negative 5-day returns more often than chance, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS indicates labor demand softening and slower household income growth, which reduces earnings expectations and weighs on S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll declines are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal is neutral after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies accelerating final demand that supports corporate revenue growth and market breadth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized GDP and retail sales strength fails to improve 20-day forward SPX returns relative to baseline or if the joint signal is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO paired with falling UNRATE signals supply normalization and better cyclical momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the recovery pattern of higher industrial production and lower unemployment does not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns in most occurrences, or if the interaction effect disappears after 1-year rolling estimation.
